questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with oaths like dawn and the ten nights?
questions.answer: To emphasize the seriousness of the message and draw attention to Allah’s signs and sacred times.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that power and civilization guarantee safety from Allah’s judgment.
questions.answer: False. It mentions powerful nations that were destroyed due to transgression.

questions.text: What is one lesson from the mention of 'Ad, Thamud, and Pharaoh?
questions.answer: When ظلم and corruption spread, Allah’s punishment can end even the strongest powers.

questions.text: What does 'your Lord is in observation' teach about hidden ظلم?
questions.answer: Allah watches and will judge; injustice is never truly unnoticed.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Fajr correct the idea that wealth means Allah honors someone?
questions.answer: It shows wealth and restriction are both tests; honor comes from obedience, not possessions.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that poverty means Allah humiliates a person.
questions.answer: False. Restriction is a test, not proof of humiliation.

questions.text: What four social sins does the Surah highlight to expose moral decay?
questions.answer: Neglecting orphans, not encouraging feeding the poor, greedy consumption of inheritance, and obsessive love of wealth.

questions.text: Why is neglecting the orphan a major spiritual failure?
questions.answer: It shows a hard heart and lack of mercy, contradicting faith and accountability.

questions.text: What is one modern example of failing to honor orphans or vulnerable people?
questions.answer: Ignoring their rights, exploiting them financially, or leaving them unsupported while having ability to help.

questions.text: How does the Surah treat love of wealth as a danger?
questions.answer: It shows excessive love of wealth leads to greed, injustice, and forgetting the Hereafter.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that inheritance is only a legal topic, not a moral one.
questions.answer: False. It criticizes greedy consumption as a moral failure.

questions.text: What does the Surah’s scene of the earth being crushed teach about dunya?
questions.answer: Worldly stability ends; the Hereafter exposes the true value of deeds.

questions.text: What does the human regret in the Surah when Hell is brought near?
questions.answer: They regret not sending forward good deeds for their real life.

questions.text: How can a believer avoid that regret practically?
questions.answer: Repent now, fulfill obligations, restore rights, and build consistent charity and prayer.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that regret alone on Judgment Day can change the outcome.
questions.answer: False. Regret without earlier repentance comes too late.

questions.text: What is the 'soul at peace' (nafs mutma'innah) described at the end?
questions.answer: A righteous soul satisfied with Allah, steady in faith, and ready to return to Him.

questions.text: How does a person develop a tranquil soul over time?
questions.answer: Through faith, consistent worship, repentance, trust in Allah, and good character.

questions.text: What does the call 'return to your Lord, pleased and pleasing' teach about the best ending?
questions.answer: The best ending is to meet Allah with acceptance, having lived in a way that pleases Him.

questions.text: How can this Surah guide someone who feels proud of their success?
questions.answer: It warns that success is a test; gratitude and justice prove faith, not pride.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that mercy to the poor is optional extra credit.
questions.answer: False. It criticizes failing to encourage feeding the poor.

questions.text: What does Allah swear by in the first two verses?
questions.answer: The Dawn and the ten nights (generally understood as the first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah).

questions.text: What are 'the even and the odd' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Scholars say it refers to all of creation (even/pairs) and the Creator (Odd/One), or specific days of Hajj.

questions.text: Who were the people of 'Iram'?
questions.answer: The people of 'Ad, who possessed lofty pillars/cities the likes of which were not created in the lands.

questions.text: What were the people of Thamud famous for?
questions.answer: Carving rocks/homes in the valley.

questions.text: How is Pharaoh described in this Surah?
questions.answer: As 'the owner of the stakes' (Dhul-Awtad).

questions.text: What did these three nations ('Ad, Thamud, Pharaoh) do in the lands?
questions.answer: They transgressed and increased corruption.

questions.text: What was the result of their corruption?
questions.answer: Allah poured upon them a 'scourge of punishment'.

questions.text: Is your Lord ever unaware of human actions?
questions.answer: No, 'Indeed, your Lord is in observation/ambush (Mirṣād)'.

questions.text: How does the 'man' react when Allah tests him with honor and blessings?
questions.answer: He says: 'My Lord has honored me'.

questions.text: How does the 'man' react when Allah tests him by restricting his provision?
questions.answer: He says: 'My Lord has humiliated me'.

questions.text: What are the four social failures Allah criticizes in this Surah?
questions.answer: Not honoring the orphan, not encouraging the feeding of the poor, consuming inheritance greedily, and loving wealth excessively.

questions.text: What will happen to the earth on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: It will be crushed with a 'crushing, crushing' (Dakkan Dakka).

questions.text: Who will come with the angels rank upon rank?
questions.answer: Your Lord (meaning His command or manifestation).

questions.text: What will be brought forth on that Day?
questions.answer: Hell (Jahannam).

questions.text: What will the human wish for when he sees Hell?
questions.answer: He will say: 'Oh, I wish I had sent forth [some good] for my life'.

questions.text: Can anyone punish like Allah's punishment or bind like His binding on that Day?
questions.answer: No.

questions.text: Who is addressed as 'An-Nafs al-Mutma'innah'?
questions.answer: The 'soul at peace' (the righteous soul).

questions.text: What is the command to the soul at peace?
questions.answer: 'Return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing [to Him]'.

questions.text: Where is the soul at peace told to enter?
questions.answer: 'Enter among My [righteous] servants and enter My Paradise'.

questions.text: What is the main message of this Surah regarding wealth?
questions.answer: Wealth is a test, not necessarily a sign of Allah's love, and its misuse leads to destruction.
